name: gitstudio
description: A free, open-source, JetBrains-grade Git suite for VS Code & Cursor — commit graph, inline blame, file & line history, hunk/line staging, side-by-side diff, 3-pane merge, interactive rebase with a universal Undo safety net, and optional bring-your-own-key AI.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open a folder with a Git repository to get started.
GitStudio shows your commits, branches, remotes, and tags here once a repository is detected.
[Open Folder](command:vscode.openFolder)
[Take the Tour](command:gitstudio.openWalkthrough)

Browse your history visually.
[Open Commit Graph](command:gitstudio.showCommitGraph)
[Take the Tour](command:gitstudio.openWalkthrough)
Open a Git repository to stage and commit changes here.

No stashes yet.
Stash your working-tree changes to set them aside without committing.
[Stash Changes](command:gitstudio.stash.save)

Work on multiple branches at once with linked worktrees.
[New Worktree](command:gitstudio.worktree.add)

Search commits or compare two refs; results show up here.
[Search Commits](command:gitstudio.search)
[Compare Branches/Tags](command:gitstudio.compareRefs)

Connect GitHub to review pull requests.
Sign in once with VS Code's built-in GitHub account to list, check out, review, merge, and create pull requests without leaving the editor.
[Sign in to GitHub](command:gitstudio.pr.signIn)

gitstudio.blame.inlineEnabled.description: Show inline blame for the current line (author, relative time, and summary) at the end of the line.

gitstudio.blame.statusBarEnabled.description: Show the current line's commit author and age in the status bar.

gitstudio.blame.heatmap.description: Tint full-file blame annotations with a code-age heatmap (recent changes warmer, older changes cooler).

gitstudio.merge.autoOpen.description: When a file becomes conflicted (during a merge, rebase, or cherry-pick), automatically open it in the GitStudio 3-pane merge editor.

gitstudio.commit.signoffByDefault.description: Enable the Sign-off toggle by default in the GitStudio commit box (adds a Signed-off-by trailer).

gitstudio.push.forceWithLease.description: When force-pushing from GitStudio, use the safer --force-with-lease (which refuses to overwrite remote work you haven't seen) instead of a raw --force.

enumDescriptions: Prefer the zero-key VS Code Language Model (GitHub Copilot) when available; otherwise use Anthropic if an API key is set.
Use Anthropic directly (requires an API key set via GitStudio: Set AI API Key).
Use the VS Code Language Model API (GitHub Copilot). Requires Copilot.
gitstudio.ai.provider.markdownDescription: Which provider GitBrain uses for optional AI features. AI is off until a provider is available; it never gates or breaks Git operations.

gitstudio.ai.anthropicModelFast.description: Anthropic model ID for fast/cheap tasks (commit messages).

gitstudio.ai.anthropicModelMid.description: Anthropic model ID for mid-tier tasks (explain diff, summaries).

gitstudio.ai.anthropicModelDeep.description: Anthropic model ID for opt-in deep reasoning.

enumDescriptions: A single concise subject line, no body.
Imperative subject plus a short explanatory body.
gitstudio.ai.commitStyle.description: How GitBrain styles generated commit messages.

enumDescriptions: Squash the PR's commits into one and merge.
Rebase the PR's commits onto the base and merge.
gitstudio.pr.defaultMergeMethod.description: Default method offered first when merging a pull request.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with GitStudio
walkthroughs.description: A free, JetBrains-grade Git suite — commit graph, blame, history, hunk staging, 3-pane merge, interactive rebase with Undo, and optional BYO-key AI. Take the tour.

steps.title: Open the Commit Graph
steps.description:
See your history as a real, virtualized branch/commit graph — fast even at tens of thousands of commits. Colored lanes, refs, and authorship, not a flat list.
[Open Commit Graph](command:gitstudio.showCommitGraph)
Tip: it also lives at the top of the GitStudio sidebar.

steps.title: Turn on inline blame
steps.description:
Ambient, native-feeling authorship: who last changed the current line and when, inline and in the status bar. Toggle full-file annotations with a code-age heatmap.
[Toggle File Blame](command:gitstudio.toggleFileBlame)
Shortcut: __Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+G B__ in any editor.

steps.title: Stage a hunk & commit
steps.description:
Stage by hunk or even by line, then commit from the GitStudio commit box (Amend, Sign-off, author override, Commit & Push). When AI is on, the ✨ button drafts a message from your staged diff.
[Open the Changes view](command:workbench.view.extension.gitstudio)
Shortcut: select lines and press __Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+G S__ to stage them.

steps.title: Explore file & line history
steps.description:
Step through how a file — or a single line — evolved over time, with revision navigation and a reflog time-machine for recovery.
[Show Line History](command:gitstudio.showLineHistory)
Shortcut: __Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+G H__ on the current line.

steps.title: Resolve conflicts in the 3-pane editor
steps.description:
When a merge, rebase, or cherry-pick conflicts, GitStudio opens a JetBrains-style three-pane editor — yours, the result, and theirs — with one-click accept ribbons. Interactive rebase gets the same care, wrapped in a universal, reflog-powered Undo.
[Start Interactive Rebase](command:gitstudio.startInteractiveRebase)
Made a mistake? __Undo Last Git Operation__ (Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+G Z) reverses it.

steps.title: Connect GitHub & set up GitBrain (optional)
steps.description:
Sign in once with VS Code's built-in GitHub account to list, check out, review, merge, and create pull requests in-editor. GitBrain AI is optional and bring-your-own-key — set an Anthropic key (or use Copilot's model) for commit messages, explain-this-diff, and summaries. It's off until you turn it on and never gates a Git operation.
[Sign in to GitHub](command:gitstudio.pr.signIn)
[Set AI API Key](command:gitstudio.ai.setApiKey)

virtualWorkspaces.description: GitStudio requires a local git repository on disk.
